Mapping Analysis: Charting a Flood

Chris Williams San Antonio Express-News May 1999
Business & Economy

Chris Williams explores the aftermath of a severe storm in San Antonio, examining why many flood-affected residents were unaware of their flood risk. The reporting reveals the correlation between damaged properties and flood plains, emphasizing the lack of flood insurance.

Two weeks after a freak storm dropped more than 15 inches of rain on San Antonio, more than 100 residents from a flood-ravaged neighborhood gathered in an elementary school gym. They wanted to know why the government, at any level, had not told them they were at risk of flooding... The path of the destruction closely followed the 100-year flood plains published in 1996. In fact, 75 percent of the 175 residences destroyed by the flood were in those flood plains.

Citywide, only about 10 percent of the damaged buildings were insured by the National Flood Insurance Program, which meant 90 percent of the people in 470 damaged buildings were trying to rebuild on slim federal grants or disaster loans they could not afford to repay.
